# Work Packages: CSV Import + Export

**Inputs**: Design documents from `kitty-specs/011-csv-import-export/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md

**Tests**: Not explicitly requested. Manual verification only.

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package is independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Precise file paths included for each subtask.

---

## Work Package WP01: Foundation — CSVParser + Service Protocols (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create the RFC 4180 CSV parser utility, define ImportService and ExportService protocols with supporting types (ImportProgress, ImportResult, ImportError), and verify the foundation compiles.
**Independent Test**: CSVParser can parse a multi-line CSV string with quoted fields containing commas and embedded newlines. Protocol types compile with no errors.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP01-foundation-csv-parser-and-protocols.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 Create CSVParser utility with RFC 4180 state machine in `Reppo/Core/Utilities/CSVParser.swift`
- [x] T002 [P] Create ImportServiceProtocol + ImportProgress/ImportResult/ImportError types in `Reppo/Core/Services/Protocols/ImportServiceProtocol.swift`
- [x] T003 [P] Create ExportServiceProtocol in `Reppo/Core/Services/Protocols/ExportServiceProtocol.swift`
- [x] T004 Add new files to Xcode project and verify build

### Implementation Notes
- CSVParser is a pure utility struct with no SwiftData dependency. Lives in `Reppo/Core/Utilities/` (create directory if needed).
- State machine parser: `inQuotes` flag, handle `""` (escaped quote), `\r\n` normalization, embedded commas/newlines inside quoted fields.
- ParseResult: `headers: [String]`, `rows: [[String]]`, `totalRows: Int`.
- PreviewResult: `headers: [String]`, `sampleRows: [[String]]`, `estimatedTotalRows: Int`.
- ValidationError: `rowNumber: Int`, `reason: String`, `rawLine: String?`.
- ImportProgress enum: `.parsing`, `.validating(processed:total:)`, `.importing(inserted:total:)`, `.rebuilding(phase:)`, `.completed(ImportResult)`, `.failed(ImportError)`.
- ImportError enum: `.fileReadFailed`, `.invalidEncoding`, `.invalidHeader`, `.noValidRows`, `.cancelled`.
- All types must be `Sendable` for actor isolation compatibility.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001 (CSVParser) and T002/T003 (protocols) are fully independent files.

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- CSVParser edge cases: trailing newline producing empty row (guard in final flush), `""` escape vs closing quote (peek-ahead logic).
- Import types must match what ImportService will produce — follow data-model.md exactly.

---

## Work Package WP02: Import Logic — ImportService Implementation (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Build the ImportService actor that orchestrates CSV import: validates rows, maps fields, groups by date, creates Workouts/Exercises/WorkoutSets in chunked batches, and triggers StatsService + PRService rebuild.
**Independent Test**: Given a CSV Data blob with known rows, ImportService.importCSV(data:) produces correct Workouts (one per date), matches/creates Exercises, creates WorkoutSets with correct field mapping, and triggers rebuild.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP02-import-service.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~550 lines
**Dependencies**: WP01

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T005 Create ImportService actor scaffold with dependency injection in `Reppo/Core/Services/ImportService.swift`
- [x] T006 Implement CSV header validation + row-level validation with error collection
- [x] T007 Implement Kind → trackingType mapping table + new Exercise defaults
- [x] T008 Implement exercise matching (case-insensitive lookup via ExerciseRepository) + Workout grouping by date
- [x] T009 Implement chunked batch insert with effectiveWeight/e1RM computation + AsyncStream progress reporting
- [x] T010 Wire ImportService into ServiceContainer + post-import rebuild trigger (StatsService.rebuildAll + PRService.rebuildAll)

### Implementation Notes
- ImportService is an `actor` conforming to `ImportServiceProtocol`.
- Dependencies: WorkoutRepository, ExerciseRepository, WorkoutSetRepository, BodyweightEntryRepository, HealthProfileRepository, PRService, StatsService, ModelContainer.
- Validation: header must contain all 11 expected columns. Per-row: Date + Exercise required, at least one data value.
- Kind mapping: `"wr"` → `.weightReps`, `"d"` → `.duration`, `"wd"` → `.weightDistance`, `"wrd"` → `.weightRepsDuration`, unknown → `.weightReps`.
- New exercises: `trackingType` from Kind (first occurrence), `equipmentType = .other`, `primaryMuscle` from Category.
- Workout grouping: one Workout per unique Date, `status = .completed`.
- WorkoutSet defaults: `setType = .working`, `completed = true`, `cachedPRStatus = nil`. Compute `effectiveWeight` and `e1RM`.
- Chunked insert: ~500 sets per batch, `modelContext.save()` after each chunk.
- Date parsing: `Date.ISO8601FormatStyle().year().month().day().dateSeparator(.dash)` — Sendable, safe in actor.
- Use `@ModelActor` pattern OR create background `ModelContext` from `ModelContainer` for batch inserts.
- After all inserts: call `StatsService.rebuildAll()` then `PRService.rebuildAll()`.
- NO per-set PR pipeline during import (AGENT_RULES Section 9).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T006 (validation) and T007 (mapping) are independent logic blocks.
- T008 through T010 are sequential (each builds on previous).

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (CSVParser, ImportServiceProtocol, types).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Memory pressure with 12k rows: chunked inserts (500/batch) mitigate this.
- Exercise name matching: must be case-insensitive to avoid duplicates.
- trackingType immutability: only set on NEW exercises. Existing exercises keep their trackingType.
- effectiveWeight computation: for imported exercises with bodyweightFactor=0 (default), effectiveWeight = weight.
- ModelContainer access: may need to expose from RepositoryContainer if not already public.

---

## Work Package WP03: Import UI — ImportView + ImportViewModel (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build the full import user experience: file picker, data preview, progress indicator, result summary, and error reporting. The view follows a state machine pattern (idle → previewing → importing → rebuilding → completed → failed).
**Independent Test**: Open ImportView, select a CSV file, see preview with first 5 rows, tap Import, see progress updating, see final result summary with counts.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP03-import-ui.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines
**Dependencies**: WP01, WP02

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T011 Create ImportViewModel with state machine in `Reppo/Features/Settings/ViewModels/ImportViewModel.swift`
- [x] T012 Create ImportView layout with state-driven UI in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/ImportView.swift`
- [x] T013 Implement `.fileImporter` integration with security-scoped resource access
- [x] T014 Implement preview state (first 5 rows table + column mapping summary + row count)
- [x] T015 Implement importing/rebuilding state (determinate ProgressView, running counts, phase label)
- [x] T016 Implement completed/failed state (summary card, error list, Done/Try Again buttons)

### Implementation Notes
- ImportViewModel is `@Observable`. State enum: `.idle`, `.previewing`, `.importing`, `.rebuilding`, `.completed`, `.failed`.
- ImportViewModel depends on ImportService (injected via init).
- File picker: `.fileImporter(isPresented:allowedContentTypes:[.commaSeparatedText])`.
- Security-scoped access: `url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource()` → read `Data(contentsOf: url)` → `defer { stop }`.
- CRITICAL: Must read file data within the security scope, then pass `Data` to ImportService (not URL).
- Preview: call `ImportService.previewCSV(data:)` → show header row, first 5 data rows in a scrollable table, total row count.
- Import: call `ImportService.importCSV(data:)` → consume AsyncStream, update progress.
- Progress: determinate `ProgressView(value: fraction)` + "Processing row X of Y..." text.
- Rebuilding: indeterminate `ProgressView` + "Rebuilding stats..." / "Rebuilding PRs..." text.
- Completed: summary card (sets imported, workouts created, exercises created, rows skipped) + [Done] button.
- Failed: error message + [Try Again] + [Cancel] buttons.
- Dark mode: use DesignTokens colors (bg, surfaceSecondary, textPrimary, textSecondary).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T014, T015, T016 (state views) are independent sections of the same file but should be built sequentially for coherent layout.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (types) and WP02 (ImportService).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Security-scoped resource: must read data before stopping access. Store as `Data`, not `URL`.
- Large preview: only show first 5 rows. Horizontal scrolling for wide tables.
- Progress: AsyncStream consumption must happen on a background Task, UI updates on MainActor.

---

## Work Package WP04: Export — ExportService + UI (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build CSV export: ExportService generates CSV data from all workouts/exercises/sets, ExportView provides a trigger button and share sheet via ShareLink.
**Independent Test**: Tap Export, CSV generates with correct header and all data rows, share sheet appears, file can be saved/shared.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP04-export.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines
**Dependencies**: WP01

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T017 Create ExportService actor in `Reppo/Core/Services/ExportService.swift`
- [x] T018 Implement CSV generation with proper field formatting and quoting
- [x] T019 Implement reverse Kind mapping (Exercise.trackingType → Kind column value)
- [x] T020 Create ExportViewModel in `Reppo/Features/Settings/ViewModels/ExportViewModel.swift`
- [x] T021 Create ExportView with ShareLink + CSVFile Transferable in `Reppo/Features/Settings/Views/ExportView.swift`

### Implementation Notes
- ExportService is an `actor` conforming to `ExportServiceProtocol`.
- Dependencies: WorkoutRepository, ExerciseRepository, WorkoutSetRepository.
- CSV generation: fetch all WorkoutSets sorted by date ASC → exercise name → orderInWorkout. Join with Workout (for date) and Exercise (for name, category, trackingType).
- Output header: `Date,Exercise,Category,Weight (kg),Weight (lbs),Reps,Distance,Distance Unit,Time,Notes,Kind`.
- Weight (lbs) column: computed from weight × 2.20462, formatted to 2 decimal places. Empty if weight is nil.
- Quoting: fields containing commas, quotes, or newlines must be enclosed in double quotes. Internal quotes escaped as `""`.
- Reverse Kind mapping: `.weightReps` → `"wr"`, `.duration` → `"d"`, `.weightDistance` → `"wd"`, `.weightRepsDuration` → `"wrd"`, `.custom` → `"wr"` (default).
- ExportViewModel is `@Observable`: `isExporting: Bool`, `exportData: Data?`, `errorMessage: String?`.
- CSVFile: struct conforming to `Transferable` with `FileRepresentation(exportedContentType: .commaSeparatedText)` + `ProxyRepresentation` fallback (iOS 17 Files app bug workaround).
- ExportView: description text, [Export] button, ProgressView while generating, ShareLink when data ready.
- Wire ExportService into ServiceContainer.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T017/T018/T019 (ExportService) and T020/T021 (UI) are independent files.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (ExportServiceProtocol).
- Independent of WP02/WP03 (import).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Large dataset export: ~12k rows should be fast (seconds, not minutes). No streaming needed.
- ShareLink iOS 17 bug: ProxyRepresentation fallback handles Files app issue.
- Memory: building full CSV string for 12k rows is ~2-5MB. Acceptable for in-memory.

---

## Work Package WP05: Integration — Settings Wiring + Verification (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Wire ImportView and ExportView into the Settings DATA section, removing the "Coming Soon" stubs. Verify full round-trip (export → re-import) and build correctness.
**Independent Test**: Open Settings → DATA section, tap Import → ImportView opens, tap Export → ExportView opens. Export data, re-import the exported CSV — data matches.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP05-integration.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~300 lines
**Dependencies**: WP02, WP03, WP04

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T022 Replace "Coming Soon" import stub in SettingsView with NavigationLink to ImportView
- [x] T023 Replace "Coming Soon" export stub in SettingsView with NavigationLink to ExportView
- [x] T024 Clean up SettingsViewModel — remove `showComingSoonAlert` and related alert
- [x] T025 Pass ImportService and ExportService from ServiceContainer through to Settings views
- [x] T026 Add all new files to Xcode project (`project.pbxproj`) and verify clean build
- [x] T027 Verify export → re-import round-trip produces matching data (SC-004)

### Implementation Notes
- SettingsView DATA section currently has two buttons triggering `showComingSoonAlert`. Replace with NavigationLinks.
- Import button: `NavigationLink { ImportView(importService: importService) } label: { Label("Import Data (CSV)", systemImage: "square.and.arrow.down") }`.
- Export button: `NavigationLink { ExportView(exportService: exportService) } label: { Label("Export Data (CSV)", systemImage: "square.and.arrow.up") }`.
- Remove `showComingSoonAlert` from SettingsViewModel and the `.alert` modifier from SettingsView.
- Services flow: `ServiceContainer` → `SettingsView` (via environment or init) → `ImportView` / `ExportView`.
- Verify round-trip: export all data → re-import the CSV → compare counts match.
- Ensure no regression on existing Settings functionality (units, formulas, rebuild, bodyweight log).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T022 and T023 are independent edits in the same view but can be done together.
- T024 and T025 are related cleanup.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (ImportService), WP03 (ImportView), WP04 (ExportService + ExportView).

### Risks & Mitigations
- ServiceContainer wiring: ImportService and ExportService must be accessible from SettingsView. Check how existing services are passed (likely via environment or init injection).
- `project.pbxproj` conflicts: if multiple features are in flight, pbxproj changes may conflict. Coordinate.
- Round-trip fidelity: exported CSV must be re-importable. Verify column order and formatting match.
